name: Par Four Golf
address: 9089 Santa Monica Blvd, West Hollywood, CA 90069
about: Par Four Golf is an indoor golf simulator venue located in West Hollywood, CA. The facility offers state-of-the-art technology including Trackman radar systems for swing analysis, ball-flight tracking, and performance enhancement. It provides hourly bookings for simulator play, along with memberships that offer discounted rates and after-hours access. The venue also features coaching programs from credentialed professionals, leagues, and tutorials. Services include personalized instruction with recorded swing analysis, and the use of the Trackman mobile app for tracking practice data and competing online. The facility requires all participants to sign a liability waiver before use.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to high-end Trackman technology for detailed swing and ball-flight analysis
Flexible membership options that include after-hours and discounted hourly rates
Professional coaching using advanced data for measurable improvement

food_and_drink.notes: No food allowed in hitting bays; consumption must be in designated areas.
No alcohol mentioned as available for purchase; BYOB policy not specified.

simulator_hardware.notes: Radar-based launch monitor for tracking ball and club data

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify whether club rentals are available. It is recommended to bring your own clubs or contact the venue directly.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No food or drinks are allowed in the hitting bays. Consumption must be kept in designated areas to maintain cleanliness.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not specify the duration for playing 18 holes. It is recommended to contact the venue for more information on session times.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: The website does not explicitly mention kid-friendly features. However, it does show images of a father teaching his son, suggesting it may accommodate juniors, but this is not confirmed.
